*Information about this image and its acquisition is forthcoming.*

The Iris Nebula (NGC 7023) is a blue reflection nebula surrounding a young star, in the constellation Cepheus. It lies about 1,300 light-years away, shines at an apparent magnitude of 6.8, and spans roughly 18 by 18 arcminutes. Approximate coordinates: RA 21h 01m, Dec +68° 10′.

*This description was generated by an AI assistant from general astronomical references, not from Jay's own notes on this image — treat it as a starting point, not a verified caption.*
